reasoning: Mamedyarov's rating on 2025-08-31 was 2746, ranking him 11th. Wesley So won the tournament and returned to the top 10, meaning he certainly surpassed Mamedyarov. Although Abdusattorov (7th, 2771) lost 5 games and will lose ~25 points, potentially dropping below Mamedyarov, at least 10 other players remain above Mamed. If just one other player (like Nepo or MVL) scored well enough to pass 2746, Mamedyarov will drop to 12th or lower on the September list. Because he is relatively inactive in classical chess compared to his peers, his ranking is likely to drift lower (numerically > 11) over time as younger/more active players gain rating. Therefore, the chance he sustains a rank of 11 (or better) is below 50%. Following the rule that uncertainty must increase with horizon, probabilities for further dates are pulled closer to 0.5.
